heading: Penalties for failure to declare
label: 1497 Penalties for failure to declare
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , . That section was superseded by section 497 of act , comprising this section, and repealed by section 651(a)(1) of the 1930 act.act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 49742 Stat. 964June 17, 1930A prior provision for forfeiture of any article subject to duty found in baggage, and not mentioned to the collector before whom entry was made, and for a penalty of treble the value of the article, was contained in R.S. § 2802, prior to repeal by , .act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 64242 Stat. 989

Amendments1993—Subsec. (a)(1)(A). , inserted “or transmitted” after “made”.Pub. L. 103–182, § 612(1)Subsec. (a)(2)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (A) read as follows: “if the article is a controlled substance, 1,000 percent of the value of the article; and”.Pub. L. 103–182, § 612(2)1988—Subsec. (a)(2)(A). substituted “1,000 percent” for “200 percent”.Pub. L. 100–6901986— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Any article not included in the declaration and entry as made, and, before examination of the baggage was begun, not mentioned in writing by such person, if written declaration and entry was required, or orally if written declaration and entry was not required, shall be subject to forfeiture and such person shall be liable to a penalty equal to the value of such article.”Pub. L. 99–570

children.label: A
children.text: is not included in the declaration and entry as made or transmitted; and

children.label: i
children.text: in writing by such person, if written declaration and entry was required, or

children.label: ii
children.text: orally, if written declaration and entry was not required;

children.label: A
children.text: if the article is a controlled substance, either $500 or an amount equal to 1,000 percent of the value of the article, whichever amount is greater; and

children.label: B
children.text: if the article is not a controlled substance, the value of the article.

Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, the value of any controlled substance shall, for purposes of this section, be equal to the amount determined by the Secretary in consultation with the Attorney General of the United States, to be equal to the price at which such controlled substance is likely to be illegally sold to the consumer of such controlled substance.

The Secretary and the Attorney General of the United States shall establish a method of determining the price at which each controlled substance is likely to be illegally sold to the consumer of such controlled substance.
